aiken/merkle_patricia_forestry/Neighbor.title: Neighbor
aiken/merkle_patricia_forestry/Neighbor.description: since | 1.0.0 --- | --- A neighbor node used in a proof. See [Proof](#Proof) for details.

aiken/merkle_patricia_forestry/ProofStep.title: ProofStep
aiken/merkle_patricia_forestry/ProofStep.description: since | 1.0.0 --- | --- We distinguish three kind of proof steps: Branch, Fork and Leaf. Each step contains a `skip` value which corresponds to the length of the common prefix at that particular level. The details of each level is documented [in the wiki :: Proof Format](https://github.com/aiken-lang/merkle-patricia-forestry/wiki/Proof-format).

cardano/transaction/OutputReference.title: OutputReference
cardano/transaction/OutputReference.description:
An `OutputReference` is a unique reference to an output on-chain. The `output_index`
 corresponds to the position in the output list of the transaction (identified by its id)
 that produced that output

types/denylist/MintRedeemer.title: MintRedeemer
types/denylist/MintRedeemer.description:
Mint redeemer. Admin-gated Add/Remove/Deinit; Init is nonce-gated.
 Every admin-gated action carries a reference-input index to the GS UTxO
 so the validator can read the current `admin_credential_hash` from GS datum.

types/denylist/SpendRedeemer.title: SpendRedeemer
types/denylist/SpendRedeemer.description:
Spend redeemer. Only `StateTransition` — all mutation logic delegates to
 the mint validator. No `ModifyUser` analogue: presence IS the state, there
 is nothing to toggle within a node.

types/global_state/GlobalStateDatum.title: GlobalStateDatum
types/global_state/GlobalStateDatum.description:
GlobalState datum — authenticated by the single global_state NFT.

 INVARIANT: `transfers_paused` MUST remain at field index 0. The fast
 pause-read via `is_paused` below relies on `unconstr_fields` + `head_list`
 to pull the first field without deserialising the whole datum. Reshuffling
 fields breaks that optimisation.

fields.title: member_root_hash
fields.description: Root hash of the MPF membership tree (32 bytes); empty = no members yet

types/global_state/GlobalStateSpendActio.title: GlobalStateSpendAction
types/global_state/GlobalStateSpendActio.description:
Spend actions on the global_state UTxO. Admin-gated actions read the
 current admin credential hash from the input datum — which means the
 compile-time owner parameter is gone. Rotation happens via `RotateAdmin`
 with dual-signature (old + new admin).

types/kyc_proof/AttestationProof.title: AttestationProof
types/kyc_proof/AttestationProof.description:
A KYC proof is a short-lived Ed25519 signature from a trusted issuer
 attesting that a specific user is KYC'd for a specific security token on a
 specific network, valid until a specific moment.

 Payload layout (exactly 66 bytes, big-endian ordering where applicable):

     Bytes  0 – 27  : user_pkh          (28)  — binds proof to a specific sender
     Byte      28   : user_kyc_tier     (1)   — 0x00 invalid; 0x01 User;
                                                0x02 Institutional; 0x03 vLEI
     Bytes 29 – 36  : valid_until_ms    (8)   — POSIX ms, big-endian
     Bytes 37 – 64  : security_policy_id(28)  — binds to a specific token
     Byte      65   : network_id        (1)   — 0x00 preview; 0x01 preprod;
                                                0x02 mainnet; 0x03 yaci

 Signature: 64-byte raw Ed25519 over the payload.
 Issuer vkey: 32-byte raw Ed25519 vkey — directly indexes
   `GlobalStateDatum.trusted_entity_vkeys`.

types/minting_logic_script/MintingLogicS.title: MintingLogicScriptWithdrawRedeemer
types/minting_logic_script/MintingLogicS.description:
Withdraw redeemer for the minting_logic_script.

 Under the Track 4 refactor, GS is minted at `initCompliance` (not at
 register), so GS always exists by the time any security-token mint
 runs. The earlier `GlobalStateRef` sum type (`GlobalStateInput` for
 steady-state vs `GlobalStateOutputIndex` for genesis) is gone — every
 mint now spends the GS UTxO via its input.

fields.title: global_state_input_index
fields.description:
Index into `self.inputs` — the GS UTxO being spent in this tx. Its
 spend validator runs under `MintSecurity`, which enforces the cap.

fields.title: minted_amount
fields.description: Amount minted (signed: positive for mint, negative for burn).

types/power_users/PowerUser.title: PowerUser
types/power_users/PowerUser.description:
Power users are the BAFIN protocol's operators. Each flag is independent —
 a power user holds exactly the set of permissions granted to them.

 KYC verification is no longer a power-user concern — it's delegated to
 trusted-entity-issued signed proofs (see `lib/types/kyc_proof.ak`).
 Sanctions (denylist) mutations are gated directly by the admin credential
 stored in `GlobalStateDatum`, not a PowerUser flag.

types/third_party_transfer_logic_script/.title: ThirdPartyDestinationAction
types/third_party_transfer_logic_script/.description:
One entry per unique destination (unique stake-credential among outputs
 carrying the security token). Admin provides a KYC proof for each
 destination + a denylist-absence covering-node reference index.

 Source is NOT checked — force transfer is an admin override: the source
 may be denylisted or its KYC may have expired. That's the point.

types/transfer_logic_script/TransferLogi.title: TransferLogicScriptAction
types/transfer_logic_script/TransferLogi.description:
One action per unique sender (unique stake-credential among inputs carrying
 the security token). Binds a KYC proof to that sender and a covering-node
 reference-input index proving the sender's pkh is absent from the denylist.

types/transfer_logic_script/TransferLogi.title: TransferLogicScriptDestinationAction
types/transfer_logic_script/TransferLogi.description:
One action per unique destination. Both a fresh KYC proof bound to the
 destination and a denylist-absence proof are required.

 Rationale: Swiss CO Art. 973e (DLT Act) requires the issuer to perform
 obligations only to a named creditor in the ledger; eWpG §14 requires the
 issuer to maintain a register of identified holders; and industry-standard
 regulated-securities designs (ERC-3643; CMTAT with RuleWhitelist) gate
 both sender and recipient. Requiring a destination proof on regular
 transfers matches all three.

types/transfer_logic_script/TransferLogi.title: TransferLogicScriptWithdrawRedeemer
types/transfer_logic_script/TransferLogi.description:
Transfer-logic withdraw redeemer.

 - `global_state_ref_input_index` — GS UTxO as ref input; provides pause
   flag, TEL, denylist policy id.
 - `actions_for_each_input` — one KYC proof + denylist-absence covering-node
   index per unique sender, ordered to match the aggregated-senders order.
 - `destination_actions` — one KYC proof + denylist-absence covering-node
   index per unique destination, ordered to match the aggregated-destinations
   order. Both positive (KYC) and negative (denylist absence) attestations
   required on receive.
